skill.name: regulatory-affairs-head
skill.description: Regulatory affairs guidance for HealthTech and MedTech teams planning FDA, EU MDR, ISO, and global medical device submissions.
skill.summary: Plan medical device regulatory strategy, submissions, risks, and team coordination.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: The high static findings are false positives from regulatory terms, markdown backticks, and document headings. No evidence found for malware, command execution, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection. The bundled Python helper reads and writes a local JSON tracking file, so the final risk is low.

low_findings.title: Local File Persistence in Helper Script
low_findings.description: The regulatory tracker loads and saves submission data to a local JSON file. This is normal persistence behavior, but users should run it only in an intended working directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write calls are direct and easy to verify. The context shows local JSON persistence, not exfiltration or destructive behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Python File Write
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script writes JSON data to the configured tracking file. The default target is local and predictable, but arbitrary paths should not be supplied from untrusted input.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write call is explicit. It is low risk because no network transfer, command execution, or hidden payload behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Medical device teams need clear regulatory plans before product and quality decisions become costly. This skill guides FDA, EU MDR, ISO, and global submission planning with structured team coordination.
actual_capabilities: Outline regulatory pathways for FDA 510(k), PMA, De Novo, EU MDR, and ISO certification work.
Structure submission preparation steps, timelines, dependencies, and authority communication plans.
Identify regulatory risks, mitigation actions, checkpoints, and escalation needs.
Coordinate cross-functional regulatory inputs from quality, clinical, engineering, and leadership teams.
Use included reference guides for FDA and EU MDR submission planning.
Track local submission status data with the bundled Python helper script.
limitations: It does not provide legal advice or replace qualified regulatory counsel.
It cannot submit documents to regulators or access authority portals.
It does not verify current laws, guidance, fees, or notified body capacity.
The tracker script stores data locally and does not provide enterprise access controls.

use_cases.title: Select an Initial Pathway
use_cases.description: Compare likely FDA and EU MDR routes before funding, design controls, and clinical planning decisions.

use_cases.title: Organize Submission Work
use_cases.description: Create a preparation plan with owners, evidence needs, authority interactions, and review milestones.

use_cases.title: Align Compliance Evidence
use_cases.description: Map quality system records, risk files, clinical evidence, and labeling inputs to submission needs.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Pathways
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new device
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me identify possible regulatory pathways for a [device type] in [target markets]. Include classification assumptions, key evidence needs, and major uncertainties.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Submission Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate FDA or EU MDR preparation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a submission preparation plan for [device name]. Cover required sections, owners, dependencies, authority interactions, and a realistic milestone timeline.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Regulatory Risk
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced risk review before a program gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this regulatory plan for [device type]: [paste plan]. Identify market access risks, evidence gaps, mitigation options, and escalation triggers.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Global Launch
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a global regulatory coordination plan for [device name] across [markets]. Include sequencing, documentation reuse, team governance, and post-market obligations.

output_examples.input: Plan the first regulatory steps for a Class II patient monitoring device in the United States.
output: Likely pathway: FDA 510(k), pending classification and predicate confirmation.
Early actions: confirm intended use, identify predicates, define performance testing, and request a Q-Sub if uncertainties remain.
Key inputs: risk management file, software documentation, labeling, clinical rationale, and quality system evidence.

output_examples.input: Create a team plan for EU MDR technical documentation readiness.
output: Regulatory owns the submission roadmap, notified body interactions, and conformity route confirmation.
Quality owns QMS evidence, design controls, document control, and audit readiness.
Clinical owns evaluation planning, literature review, investigation rationale, and PMCF planning.

output_examples.input: Assess risks for a delayed FDA submission program.
output: Primary risks: missing performance evidence, unclear predicate strategy, late labeling review, and unresolved software documentation.
Mitigations: hold a pathway review, lock evidence owners, schedule weekly issue review, and prepare contingency questions for FDA.

best_practices: Confirm current regulatory guidance with official sources before making submission commitments.
Document assumptions, owners, evidence gaps, and decision dates for every regulatory plan.
Use the tracker script only in a controlled project folder with non-sensitive local data.
anti_patterns: Treating AI output as final legal or regulatory advice without expert review.
Starting submission planning after design, clinical, and quality evidence are already fixed.
Putting confidential patient, employee, or trade secret data into prompts or local examples.

faq.question: What regulatory areas does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers medical device strategy for FDA pathways, EU MDR planning, ISO alignment, submissions, risks, and team coordination.

faq.question: Can it prepare a final submission package?
faq.answer: No. It can structure planning and draft checklists, but qualified experts must prepare and approve formal submissions.

faq.question: Does it check live regulatory updates?
faq.answer: No. Users should confirm current rules, guidance, fees, and timelines with official regulatory sources.

faq.question: What does the Python tracker do?
faq.answer: It stores local submission records in JSON and prints status reports for deadlines, statuses, and active reviews.

faq.question: Is this suitable for software as a medical device?
faq.answer: Yes, it includes planning prompts for software documentation, cybersecurity considerations, risk management, and lifecycle evidence.

faq.question: What data should users avoid sharing?
faq.answer: Users should avoid protected health information, confidential submissions, credentials, and trade secrets unless their environment permits it.
